title: To PRD
description: Convert a clarified requirement into a concise and testable product requirements document.
triggers: The requirement has been clarified and needs an approval-ready specification.

inputs.description: Clarified problem, users, constraints, and open decisions.
UI states and interaction contract when the work affects user interfaces.

approval.reason: The PRD becomes the approved scope for planning and implementation.

completionCriteria: The PRD contains scope, non-goals, observable acceptance criteria, constraints, and open questions.
Assumptions are marked and unresolved material decisions are not treated as approved.
stopConditions: The requirement still has a material product, data, permission, or UX ambiguity.
Repository evidence contradicts the requested behavior.
